questions.question: Which statement about the factors of production is correct? A Capital is a financial resource and includes savings in banks., B Enterprise is limited in supply and includes office workers., C Labour is a human resource and includes pensioners and children., D Land is a natural resource and includes trees and fish.
questions.text: Which statement about the factors of production is correct? A Capital is a financial resource and includes savings in banks., B Enterprise is limited in supply and includes office workers., C Labour is a human resource and includes pensioners and children., D Land is a natural resource and includes trees and fish.
options: A Capital is a financial resource and includes savings in banks.
B Enterprise is limited in supply and includes office workers.
C Labour is a human resource and includes pensioners and children.
D Land is a natural resource and includes trees and fish.

questions.question: A builder spends one hour travelling to and from work. He works 40 hours per week for $200. He is given a choice to work 50 hours a week for an extra $75. What would be the opportunity cost to the builder of working the extra hours? A the ability to increase his purchasing power, B the additional time travelling to and from work, C the increased hourly earnings of $7.50, D the ten hours of leisure time
questions.text: A builder spends one hour travelling to and from work. He works 40 hours per week for $200. He is given a choice to work 50 hours a week for an extra $75. What would be the opportunity cost to the builder of working the extra hours? A the ability to increase his purchasing power, B the additional time travelling to and from work, C the increased hourly earnings of $7.50, D the ten hours of leisure time
options: A the ability to increase his purchasing power
B the additional time travelling to and from work
C the increased hourly earnings of $7.50
D the ten hours of leisure time

questions.question: A  country’s  infrastructure  was  damaged  by  a  natural  disaster. Before  the  disaster,  the  country was producing at point Y on the production possibility curve (PPC). Which point represents the country’s production immediately after the disaster? capital goods consumer goods O B C A D Y

questions.question: What is included in microeconomics? A the calculation of national income and expenditure, B the effects of a change in exchange rates on the trade balance of an economy, C the effects of an increase in the price of a good or service, D the factors leading to demand-pull inflation 3
questions.text: What is included in microeconomics? A the calculation of national income and expenditure, B the effects of a change in exchange rates on the trade balance of an economy, C the effects of an increase in the price of a good or service, D the factors leading to demand-pull inflation 3
options: A the calculation of national income and expenditure
B the effects of a change in exchange rates on the trade balance of an economy
C the effects of an increase in the price of a good or service
D the factors leading to demand-pull inflation 3

questions.question: What is an economist’s definition of a market? A a collection of informal retail outlets run by small firms, B a place where traders gather regularly for short periods, C a system that enables individuals and firms to buy and sell goods, D the existence of consumer demand for a good

B a place where traders gather regularly for short periods
C a system that enables individuals and firms to buy and sell goods
D the existence of consumer demand for a good

A higher cost of building cycle lanes, B improved physical fitness to the individual from cycling, C lower cost of cycling compared with driving to work, D reduced congestion for other road users

questions.question: It is expected that consumers will use less paper money. What is the most likely reason for this? A Inflation reduces the value of paper money., B More ways of making payments will be available., C Other forms of money are more durable., D Paper money will have less intrinsic value.
questions.text: It is expected that consumers will use less paper money. What is the most likely reason for this? A Inflation reduces the value of paper money., B More ways of making payments will be available., C Other forms of money are more durable., D Paper money will have less intrinsic value.
options: A Inflation reduces the value of paper money.
B More ways of making payments will be available.
C Other forms of money are more durable.
D Paper money will have less intrinsic value.

questions.question: In low-income countries, fewer girls than boys become skilled design engineers. What might explain this situation? A Girls are only interested in low-wage jobs., B Girls do not favour heavy manual work., C Girls have fewer opportunities to complete higher education., D It costs more for girls to be trained as design engineers.

options: A Girls are only interested in low-wage jobs.
B Girls do not favour heavy manual work.

D It costs more for girls to be trained as design engineers.

questions.question: A company mining cobalt, invests in machinery to replace some workers. Eventually diseconomies of scale occur. What could cause this situation? A As more cobalt is mined average costs increase., B Fewer workers are needed to produce the required output., C Output per hour increases as more machinery is used., D Overhead costs are spread as output increases.

options: A As more cobalt is mined average costs increase.
B Fewer workers are needed to produce the required output.
C Output per hour increases as more machinery is used.
D Overhead costs are spread as output increases.

questions.question: Dimitry owns a firm that produces and sells bottles of lemonade. He only sells one size of bottle. How would Dimitry calculate the total revenue of the firm? A multiply the quantity sold by the cost per bottle, B multiply the quantity sold by the price per bottle, C multiply the quantity sold by the profit per bottle, D multiply the quantity sold by the tax per bottle

questions.question: An economy has a high rate of inflation. In response to this, its government increases income tax. What is the most likely reason for this increase? A to discourage the consumption of harmful goods, B to raise money for government spending, C to redistribute income, D to reduce total demand
questions.text: An economy has a high rate of inflation. In response to this, its government increases income tax. What is the most likely reason for this increase? B to raise money for government spending

D to reduce total demand

questions.question: Interest rates are sometimes raised to control inflation. Why might this policy be effective? A Consumers may save more., B Government spending may increase., C Investment may be encouraged., D The exchange rate may fall.
questions.text: Interest rates are sometimes raised to control inflation. Why might this policy be effective? A Consumers may save more., B Government spending may increase., C Investment may be encouraged., D The exchange rate may fall.
options: A Consumers may save more.
B Government spending may increase.
C Investment may be encouraged.
D The exchange rate may fall.

questions.question: A government’s revenue grew 8% to $3.25 trillion. Its expenditure was reduced to $3.69 trillion. What can be concluded from this? state of budget government policy aim A deficit increase deficit, B deficit reduce deficit, C surplus increase surplus, D surplus reduce surplus
questions.text: A government’s revenue grew 8% to $3.25 trillion. Its expenditure was reduced to $3.69 trillion. What can be concluded from this? state of budget government policy aim A deficit increase deficit, B deficit reduce deficit, C surplus increase surplus, D surplus reduce surplus
options: A deficit increase deficit
B deficit reduce deficit
C surplus increase surplus
D surplus reduce surplus

questions.question: A  country’s  inflation  rate,  measured  by  the  Consumer  Prices  Index  (CPI),  was  3%  in  year  1. Three years later it was 0.8%. What can be concluded from this information? A Prices are falling., B The rate of price increases is falling., C The real rate of interest is negative., D There is increased purchasing power for those on fixed incomes. 7

questions.question: What is a disadvantage of increasing international specialisation?
